Generate your professional resume, portfolio, academic history, and GitHub profile — all from a single configuration file.

Templates support internationalization o: Templates support internationalization out of the box.
why description:
Most developers waste hours maintaining separate resumes, portfolios, and GitHub profiles. Devsync solves this by generating everything from a single source of truth. One file, infinite outputs. No more copy-pasting, no more out-of-sync information.

Your career evolves constantly. New projects, new skills, new certifications. Keeping every platform updated is a full-time job. Devsync automates this by reading your DEVSYNC.json and producing polished, professional outputs across every channel that matters.

Devsync is also for backend developers, data scientists, DevOps engineers, students, career changers, and anyone who wants a professional web portfolio without coding or researching web technologies. Not just a portfolio—an ATS-ready CV that passes automated filters without studying Harvard's formatting guidelines. Great job-search documentation includes many details, and Devsync consolidates it all into a single file.

Focus on what you do best — building. Let Devsync handle the rest. From ATS-friendly resumes to stunning portfolio sites, everything stays in perfect harmony with zero manual effort.
creator description:
Devsync started as a personal script to solve a problem every developer knows: updating the same information across five different platforms. Resume, portfolio, GitHub, LinkedIn, academic records — different formats, same content, endless repetition.

I built it for myself first. Then I realized this could help others too. So I open-sourced it. No paywalls, no accounts, no friction. Just one file that generates everything you need.
templates description: Start with a polished template that looks great out of the box. Pick one and make it your own. Or read more about building your own template.
why security: Security is a top priority. Devsync uses Bun as its runtime and package manager. You don't need to worry about package vulnerabilities — the `bunfig.toml` configuration includes `minimumReleaseAge = 259200` (3 days), which prevents newly published packages from being installed automatically. This protects against supply chain attacks.
get started description: Start with a ready-made template or build your own from scratch. Both paths give you full control.
use template description: Pick from our curated templates and start building your portfolio in minutes. Perfect for getting started quickly.
create from scratch description: Build a custom template with any framework you want. Get the core structure and full flexibility to make it yours.
use with ai description: Let AI build your portfolio for you. Devsync includes an AI skill that understands how to work with your templates.
